ingredients.name: TAMASHII NATIONS STORE TOKYO
ingredients.address: TAMASHII NATIONS STORE TOKYO, １-1 Kanda Hanaokacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0028, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Fans visit this hobby store in Akihabara in honor of the Love Live! merchandise culture that exploded with Season 2, where the girls hustle to sell out their concert goods to fund their school.

ingredients.name: Myojin Otoko-zaka Stair
ingredients.address: Kanda Myojin Otoko-zaka, 2 Chome-7-4 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This steep stone staircase beside Kanda Myojin Shrine appears in Episode 1 of Season 2 as the girls run up it during their morning training, a recurring motif showing the dedication of μ's.

ingredients.name: El Chateo del Puente
ingredients.localName: エルチャテオ デルプエンテ
ingredients.address: El Chateo del Puente, ＪＲ紅梅橋高架下 １号 2 Chome-９番地 Kanda Awajicho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Spanish restaurant under the railway bridge in Kanda appears in Episode 8 as a background building when characters walk through the Awajicho neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Electric Town
ingredients.address: Akihabara Electric Town, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The main street of Akihabara appears in Episode 6 when the girls walk through the electric town district, the spiritual home of idol culture that mirrors μ's own fanbase.

ingredients.name: Daiei Park Yushima
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒113-0034 Tokyo, Bunkyo City, 9 Daiei Park Yushima, 湯島４丁目９－１２付近
ingredients.entityContext: This Yushima parking lot near Ueno appears in Episode 5 as a background location in the Bunkyo district scenes where the girls travel across Tokyo.

ingredients.name: ONODEN
ingredients.address: ONODEN, 1 Chome-2-7 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The ONODEN electronics shop facade on Akihabara's main strip appears in Episode 6 as the girls shop and explore the neighborhood that defines otaku culture in their city.

ingredients.name: Shoheibashi Higashi-zume Park
ingredients.address: Shoheibashi Higashi-zume Park, 1 Chome-1-1-1 Kanda Sudacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small riverside plaza at the east end of Shohei Bridge appears in Episode 1 during the opening sequences where μ's members walk through the Kanda district together.

ingredients.name: Kanda Myoujin Shrine
ingredients.address: Kanda Myoujin Shrine, 2 Chome-16-2 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanda Myojin is the spiritual heart of Love Live and appears prominently in Episode 1 of Season 2 as the shrine the girls visit at the start of their final year push to save Otonokizaka High School.

ingredients.name: Kozu Coast
ingredients.address: Kozu Coast, 2 Chome-6-21 Kouzu, Odawara, Kanagawa 256-0812, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kozu Coast along Sagami Bay in Odawara appears in Episode 11 during the school trip arc, where the sweeping ocean view provides a dramatic backdrop for the girls reflecting on their journey together.

ingredients.name: Senso-ji
ingredients.address: Sensō-ji, 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Senso-ji temple in Asakusa appears in Episode 11 during the school trip sequences when μ's explore Tokyo landmarks together, a poignant outing before their impending disbandment.

ingredients.name: Boat Dock, Shinobazuno Pond
ingredients.localName: 上野恩賜公園ボート場
ingredients.address: Boat Dock, Shinobazuno Pond, 2-1 Uenokoen, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shinobazuno Pond boat dock in Ueno Park appears in Episode 11 during the school trip, where the girls enjoy a leisurely boat ride as a rare moment of relaxation amid their final year pressures.

ingredients.name: West Garden Cafeteria
ingredients.localName: 西園休憩所 上野動物園
ingredients.entityContext: The West Garden rest area inside Ueno Zoo appears in Episode 11 as part of the school trip day the girls spend together touring Ueno, a gentle slice-of-life moment for μ's.

ingredients.name: Shinobazu-no-ike Pond
ingredients.address: Shinobazu Pond, 5-20 Uenokoen, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0007, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shinobazu Pond in Ueno Park appears in Episode 10 as a tranquil setting where the girls walk and talk, reflecting the bittersweet mood of the season as their final Love Live competition draws near.

ingredients.name: Gyoko-Dori Ave.
ingredients.address: Gyoko-Dori Ave., Chiyoda City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The grand imperial boulevard running from Tokyo Station toward the Imperial Palace appears in Episode 9 as a sweeping city backdrop used during the Bokura no LIVE Kimi to no LIFE performance sequence.

ingredients.name: Kanda Bridge
ingredients.address: Kanda Bridge, 1 Chome-3 Uchikanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0047, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanda Bridge over the outer moat appears in Episode 9 during Honoka's city-crossing journey, a quiet bridge walk that underscores the emotional weight of her decisions that episode.

ingredients.name: General Insurance Building
ingredients.entityContext: The Sonpo Kaikan building near Awajicho appears in Episode 8 as a background structure in the Kanda district street scenes when characters navigate the neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Takemura
ingredients.address: Takemura, 1 Chome-１９ Kanda Sudacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic Showa-era confectionery shop in Kanda Sudacho appears in Episode 8 as an atmospheric backdrop, its old-fashioned wooden exterior matching the nostalgic tone of scenes set in this part of the city.

ingredients.name: Brillia Akihabara
ingredients.localName: ブリリア秋葉原（Brillia秋葉原）
ingredients.address: Brillia Akihabara, 4 Chome-12-11 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This condominium tower in northern Akihabara appears in Episode 4 as a background landmark during street scenes as the girls move through the Sotokanda area near their beloved Akiba haunts.

ingredients.name: Times Sotokanda 4-chome No.3
ingredients.localName: トレーディア 秋葉原本店
ingredients.address: Card Shop BEAMS Akihabara, MNビル 4階 3 Chome-15-5 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This street corner in Sotokanda near the Akihabara district appears in Episode 4 as a recognizable background location during the girls' activities in the area.

ingredients.name: Niku No Hanamasa Akihabara
ingredients.localName: 肉のハナマサ 秋葉原店
ingredients.address: Niku No Hanamasa, 4-４番地１４ Akihabara, Taito City, Tokyo 110-0006, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Akihabara grocery store appears in Episode 4 as a background building that grounds the show's Akihabara setting in everyday Tokyo life alongside the idol culture.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Gamers
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-14-7 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Gamers flagship store appears in Episode 3 as the girls navigate the otaku shopping district, a setting that mirrors μ's own place in the idol world that Akiba represents.

ingredients.name: Akihabara UDX
ingredients.type: ビジネス センター
categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Akihabara UDX building appears in Episode 3 as a major background landmark during street-level scenes in the electric town, one of the most photographed pilgrimage backdrops in the show.

ingredients.name: Mandarake Complex
ingredients.localName: まんだらけコンプレックス
ingredients.type: コレクターズ ショップ
categories: コレクターズ ショップ
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-11-12 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mandarake Complex in Akihabara appears in Episode 4 as a background during the girls' Akiba excursion, part of the detailed recreation of the district that Love Live fans recognize instantly.

ingredients.name: Kaminarimon
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Kaminarimon thunder gate at Senso-ji in Asakusa appears in Episode 11 during the school trip, giving fans a chance to recreate the shot of the girls standing before Tokyo's most famous gate.

ingredients.name: Nebukawa Station
ingredients.address: Nebukawa, Odawara, Kanagawa 250-0024, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nebukawa, a quiet cliffside station overlooking Sagami Bay on the Tokaido Line, appears in Episode 11 during the school trip arc, its ocean panorama providing one of the season's most scenic location shots.

ingredients.name: Ueno Zoological Gardens
ingredients.address: 9-83 Uenokoen, Taito City, Tokyo 110-8711, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ueno Zoo is visited by μ's in Episode 11 during their school trip day in Ueno, giving the girls a cheerful group outing that serves as one of their last carefree moments before the final Love Live.

ingredients.name: Shinagawa Station
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-26-27 Takanawa, Minato City, Tokyo 108-0074, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shinagawa Station appears in Episode 11 as a transit hub the girls pass through during their school trip travels across Tokyo, a brief but recognized location for pilgrimage fans retracing the route.

ingredients.name: Asakusa Hanayashiki
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-28-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Japan's oldest amusement park in Asakusa appears in Episode 11 during the school trip, where the girls enjoy rides and attractions together in a joyful sequence that highlights their bond before the final chapter.

ingredients.name: BicCamera AKIBA Store
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-1-１ Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: BicCamera's large Akihabara store appears in Episode 6 as a backdrop along the main Akihabara strip, part of the show's faithful recreation of the electric town environment the girls call their neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Horin Park
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-5-18 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Horin Park in Sotokanda near Akihabara appears in Episode 8 as a local green space the characters pass through during their Kanda district scenes.

ingredients.name: Harumi-Futo
ingredients.address: Harumi-Futō, 5-chōme-8 Harumi, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0053, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Harumi Pier in Tokyo Bay appears in Episode 12 during the climactic final arc, its wide open waterfront providing a dramatic setting tied to the emotional resolution of μ's story in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Shohei Bridge
ingredients.address: Shohei Bridge, 1 Chome, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shohei Bridge over the Kanda River appears in Episode 11 as a recognizable landmark during the closing sequences, with the bridge's red railings frequently photographed by pilgrimage visitors.
